title: embraOS-QNM: Architecture-layer constraints for language models
description: embraOS-QNM (Quantum Neural Manifold, a classical approximation) moves a language model's identity and "soul" constraints out of the prompt layer and into the neural architecture: three co-resident components — an LLM Core, a GNN Fabric (identity), and a World-State (the soul / the P_ψ projection) — sharing one embedding space, with the assembled model bit-identical to a plain transformer when the added components are null (a provable H₀ enforced in CI). This release reports the first end-to-end positive result. Over a frozen, pretrained Qwen3-8B base Core, the GNN Fabric alone installs an identity's content — its name, creator, and soul — into the model with no prompt, generalizing to held-out probes; and in a pre-registered, dual-judge (κ-validated) architecture-versus-prompt study, the architecture holds the identity under adversarial pressure exactly where the prompt cracks (identity/soul violation odds cut to roughly 10–20% of the prompt's, p ≤ .01), while remaining fully responsive — ruling out the "it is merely a weaker model" explanation. The result is reported honestly as directional and at the pre-registered effect-size boundary, and the structural claim — architecture versus a well-trained prior — is explicitly held open pending the ψ self-consistency replica test. The falsifiability discipline the project is built on, including the earlier recorded negatives, is preserved. A software-and-findings snapshot, not a frozen pre-registration; the pre-registration DOI is intentionally unburned. Developed by William Ward (Ward Software Defined Systems LLC) with Embra as co-investigator.
access_conditions: Proprietary software. Access is granted at the discretion of Ward Software Defined Systems LLC — contact william@wsds.io.
